$comment: CC-WORDLIST-RAFU F3 — the per-language orthographic TRAP REGISTRY. THE single source of truth for what counts as a spelling trap in each language, and how a word gets tagged with one. Consumed by scripts/trap-tag.mjs (tagging + per-tier coverage counts). This file is DATA: auditors correct it without touching the engine, so every rule here is declarative — the tagger implements match kinds, never per-language knowledge. Adding a trap class = an entry here. Taxonomies transcribed from CC-WORDLIST-RAFU F3, extending CC-NEW-LANG-CONTENT D3's tiering.

$tagging.auto: The tagger decides from the word's own codepoints via `match`. Reliable enough to gate on.
$tagging.manual: The tagger CANNOT decide (RAFU decision 9: propose a workflow, never guess). It emits the class as UNTAGGED and lists the word for auditor judgement. `why` records exactly what information is missing. A manual class never auto-satisfies a quota.
$tagging.assisted: `match` narrows the CANDIDATE set cheaply, but membership is a human call. The tagger emits `candidate: true` and the auditor confirms. Counts toward a quota only once confirmed.

$match_kinds.contains_any: word contains at least one of `values` (single codepoints or strings)
$match_kinds.final_char_any: word's LAST character is one of `values`
$match_kinds.none: no automatic rule — manual classes only

$quotas.$comment: RAFU flags per-tier trap quotas as ERIC'S decision (F3 + decision 6): proposed in the gap report, not set here. `min_share` is null until he rules. The tagger REPORTS coverage against null as informational and cannot fail a build on it — a quota nobody approved is not a gate.

traps.name: Unstressed о/а and е/и reduction
traps.description: Unstressed о reduces toward [ɐ] and е/и merge, so вода sounds like [vɐˈda]. The single most common Russian spelling error class.
traps.why: Requires STRESS POSITION, which a surface-form lexicon does not carry. 'вода' is a trap; 'роза' (stressed о) is not — identical letters, opposite verdicts. Auto-tagging on 'contains о' would tag nearly every Russian word and mean nothing.
traps.workflow: Either (a) source a stress-marked lexicon and promote this to auto, or (b) auditor tags during curation. At ~40-50 words/tier this is minutes of work, not a blocker.

traps.name: ь vs ъ
traps.description: Neither sign is audible; the writer must know it is there. мышь, дверь, объект.

traps.name: -ться vs -тся
traps.description: Homophonous verb endings distinguished only by grammar (infinitive vs 3rd person). учиться / учится.

traps.name: ё
traps.description: Canonical forms store ё (CC-LINEUP-SWAP D4); typed е is ACCEPTED and ё is displayed on reveal. Tagged so ё coverage is measurable, since the source's ё fidelity is the open question in docs/russian-source-options.md.

traps.name: Doubled consonants
traps.description: класс, группа, аллея — the doubling is inaudible in Russian.

traps.name: Voiced/devoiced finals (б/п, д/т, г/к, в/ф, ж/ш, з/с)
traps.description: A word-final voiced obstruent devoices: друг → [druk], зуб → [zup]. The ear hears the wrong letter.

traps.name: ч/щ and ш/ж confusions
traps.description: The hushing sibilants are close enough that learners and native writers alike swap them.
traps.why: Presence of a hushing letter makes a word a CANDIDATE, but whether it is genuinely confusable depends on the word (щука is not a trap; счастье is). Auto-tagging on presence would over-count heavily.

traps.name: Hamza seats (أ إ ئ ؤ ء)
traps.description: Which seat carries the hamza follows rules keyed to surrounding vowels — the core difficulty engine of Arabic spelling.
traps.why: RAFU decision 9 names this explicitly. Detecting that a word CONTAINS a hamza is trivial; deciding whether its seat is CORRECT requires the vowel context that unvocalized orthography deletes. The tagger must never assert correctness.
traps.workflow: Tagger flags hamza-bearing candidates; the native auditor rules on seat correctness. Do not gate a quota on the unconfirmed candidate count.

traps.name: ة vs ه (final)
traps.description: Final taa marbuta and haa are near-homophones in pause; the choice is grammatical.

traps.name: ى vs ي (final)
traps.description: Final alif maqsura and yaa are visually and phonetically confusable.

traps.name: Madda (آ)
traps.description: آ is a LETTER, not a diacritic — it survives the unvocalized standard and RAFU F4's tashkeel rejection.

traps.name: Dagger-alif words
traps.description: هذا, رحمن, لكن — an alif pronounced but not written in the unvocalized form.
traps.why: The dagger alif itself (U+0670) is a DIACRITIC, which RAFU F4 rejects from stored forms. So in the unvocalized orthography we ship, these words carry no distinguishing mark at all — هذا is just ه-ذ-ا. Membership is lexical knowledge, not a pattern.
traps.workflow: Auditor supplies the lexical set; consider promoting to auto via a data list of known dagger-alif words once the auditor provides one.

traps.name: Sun-letter assimilation
traps.description: الشمس is written with ل but pronounced ash-shams — spelling and sound diverge.
traps.why: The pattern only fires on words carrying the ال article. Lexicons list lemmas WITHOUT the article, so this will under-match badly on a raw source — an empty count here means the source shape, not an absence of traps.
traps.workflow: Auditor decides whether the curated list should carry article-bearing forms at all; if not, retire this class or re-scope it.

fa.name: Persian
fa.script: Arabic (Persian letters — ی U+06CC, ک U+06A9; never Arabic ي/ك)

traps.name: س / ص / ث
traps.description: All three are [s] in Tehrani Persian. Persian's defining spelling challenge: the letter is unrecoverable from the sound.
traps.note: Matches the MARKED members (ص/ث) only. Plain س is the unmarked default and would fire on a large share of the lexicon while carrying no difficulty.

traps.name: ز / ذ / ض / ظ
traps.description: All four are [z] in Persian.
traps.note: Marked members only; plain ز is the unmarked default.

traps.name: ت / ط
traps.description: Both are [t].

traps.name: ه / ح
traps.description: Both are [h].

traps.name: غ / ق
traps.description: Merged to a single phoneme in Tehrani pronunciation.

traps.name: Silent و (خوا-)
traps.description: خواهر, خواب — the و is written and not pronounced.

traps.name: ZWNJ compounds
traps.description: Words whose correct written form requires U+200C ZERO WIDTH NON-JOINER.
traps.why: RAFU decision 7: CC-RTL D7's ZWNJ policy is still PROPOSED (store canonical ZWNJ, accept input without it — exactly the ё pattern). Until Eric confirms, these words are HELD in staging under this tag and their count is reported, never merged.
traps.workflow: Report the count in the gap report; merge only after the ZWNJ policy is decided.

traps.name: Final ه vs ـه
traps.description: Final heh distinctions (silent/vowel-carrying vs consonantal).
traps.why: Final ه is extremely common; whether it is a TRAP depends on its function in that word. Presence alone would over-count.

traps.name: س / ص / ث
traps.description: The Perso-Arabic homophone classes as they apply to Urdu.

traps.name: ز / ذ / ض / ظ
traps.description: All [z] in Urdu.

traps.name: ت / ط
traps.description: Both [t].

traps.name: ہ / ح
traps.description: Both [h].

traps.name: غ / ق
traps.description: Confusable in Urdu pronunciation.

traps.name: ھ (do-chashmi he) vs ہ (gol he)
traps.description: do-chashmi he marks aspiration digraphs (بھ, پھ, تھ); gol he is the standalone [h]. Distinct codepoints, easily confused.

traps.name: ں (nun ghunna)
traps.description: Nasalization carried by a dotless nun — distinct from ن.

traps.name: Retroflex ٹ ڈ ڑ
traps.description: Retroflex vs dental — the ٹ/ت, ڈ/د, ڑ/ر contrasts.

traps.name: ے (bari ye) vs ی
traps.description: Bari ye appears word-finally for certain vowels; choosing between ے and ی is a written-form decision.

traps.name: ع-initial words
traps.description: Initial ain is silent-to-vocalic in Urdu; its presence is unrecoverable from the sound.
